subject: ri_policy_router
summary: Even inside fixed mixed local windows, blocked-versus-substituted balance is phase-sensitive rather than universal: blocked rows look locally stronger in the mixed March 2020 window but locally weaker in the recurrent hostile March 2018 window.
mechanism: The two cohorts do not fire row-for-row inside the same bounded interval. In March 2020 continuation rows carry the earlier left-tail segment and blocked rows appear later in the sequence, while the March 2018 window has a different local ordering and different balance. This constrains same-window evidence to chronology and phase structure rather than universal cohort ranking.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat same-window overlap as row-matched proof of universal blocked-versus-substituted superiority.
Do not widen to more same-window subjects before resolving phase ordering on the existing 2020 and 2018 windows.
Do not use these two fixed windows as runtime tuning or promotion authority.
next_admissible_step: If this line is reopened, quantify phase ordering on the same fixed 2020 and 2018 windows only rather than widening to more years or returning to runtime tuning.
